records.ety: [L. aequilibrium, fr. aequilibris in equilibrium, level; aequus equal + libra balance. See Equal, and Librate.]

defs: Equality of weight or force; an equipoise or a state of rest produced by the mutual counteraction of two or more forces.
A level position; a just poise or balance in respect to an object, so that it remains firm; equipoise; as, to preserve the equilibrium of the body.
quotes: Health consists in the equilibrium between those two powers.

defs: A balancing of the mind between motives or reasons, with consequent indecision and doubt.
